   139→================================================================================
   140→KEY LEARNINGS & IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS
   141→================================================================================
   142→
   143→## 1. participantId Handling Pattern
   144→
   145→LEARNING: The `getParticipantIdFromToken()` function returns Promise<string | undefined>,
   146→not a synchronous value. This caused TypeScript errors initially.
   147→
   148→SOLUTION: Move participantId retrieval to the SERVICE layer, not the API layer.
   149→The API layer should accept participantId as a parameter.
   150→
   151→CORRECT PATTERN:
   152→```typescript
   153→// In service (products.service.ts):
   154→const participantId = await getParticipantIdFromToken();
   155→const response = await pointsProductsApi.getProducts(participantId);
   156→
   157→// In API (products.api.ts):
   158→async getProducts(participantId?: string): Promise<PointsProductsApiResponse> {
   159→  const response = await api.get<PointsProductsApiResponse>(
   160→    `/fintech/v1/rewardProgram/productslist`,
   161→    { headers: { ...(participantId && { participantId }) } }
   162→  );
   163→  return response.data;
   164→}
   165→```
   166→
   167→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   168→
   169→## 2. Status Enum Pattern vs Boolean Flags
   170→
   171→LEARNING: Redux uses isLoading/isError booleans. Zustand in this project uses Status enums.
   172→
   173→Pattern conversion:
   174→  - isLoading: true  -> status: Status.LOADING
   175→  - isError: true    -> status: Status.ERROR
   176→  - success state    -> status: Status.SUCCESS
   177→  - initial state    -> status: Status.IDLE
   178→
   179→Benefits:
   180→  - Single source of truth for state
   181→  - Cleaner conditional logic
   182→  - Easier to add new states (e.g., REFRESHING)
   183→
   184→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   185→
   186→## 3. Callback Handling in Rescue Domain
   187→
   188→LEARNING: Redux rescue action accepts callbacks (onSuccess, onFailed) in the action payload.
   189→These callbacks were handled by Redux-Saga middleware.
   190→
   191→SOLUTION: In Zustand, handle callbacks in the SERVICE layer, not the store.
   192→
   193→```typescript
   194→// rescue.service.ts
   195→export async function performRescue(
   196→  payload: RescuePayload,
   197→  callbacks?: RescueCallbacks
   198→): Promise<void> {
   199→  try {
   200→    rescueActions.setStatus(RescueStatus.LOADING);
   201→    const response = await rescueApi.rescue(payload);
   202→
   203→    if (response?.data?.status === 'OK') {
   204→      rescueActions.setStatus(RescueStatus.SUCCESS);
   205→      callbacks?.onSuccess?.(response.status);  // Callback here
   206→    } else {
   207→      rescueActions.setStatus(RescueStatus.ERROR);
   208→      callbacks?.onFailed?.(response?.status);   // Callback here
   209→    }
   210→  } catch (error) {
   211→    rescueActions.setStatus(RescueStatus.ERROR);
   212→    callbacks?.onFailed?.();
   213→  }
   214→}
   215→```
   216→
   217→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   218→
   219→## 4. Store Slice Composition
   220→
   221→LEARNING: All slices must be added to:
   222→  1. RewardsProgramRootStore type (intersection type)
   223→  2. setRewardsProgramSlices function
   224→  3. clearRewardsProgramStore cleanup function
   225→
   226→Final slices.ts structure:
   227→```typescript
   228→export type RewardsProgramRootStore =
   229→  ParameterPointsProgramSlice &
   230→  PermissionSlice &
   231→  AirlineMilesSlice &
   232→  PointsExtractSlice &
   233→  PointsProductsSlice &
   234→  RescueSlice &
   235→  MarketplaceSlice;
   236→```
   237→
   238→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   239→
   240→## 5. Cleanup Function - ALL Slices Must Be Cleared
   241→
   242→CRITICAL: The clearRewardsProgramStore() function must reset ALL slices.
   243→Missing any slice will cause state persistence issues across sessions.
   244→
   245→Final cleanup.ts:
   246→```typescript
   247→export function clearRewardsProgramStore() {
   248→  const {
   249→    parameterPointsProgramActions,
   250→    rewardPermissionActions,
   251→    airlineMilesActions,
   252→    pointsExtractActions,
   253→    pointsProductsActions,
   254→    rescueActions,
   255→    marketplaceActions,
   256→  } = useRootStore.getState();
   257→
   258→  parameterPointsProgramActions.reset();
   259→  rewardPermissionActions.reset();
   260→  airlineMilesActions.reset();
   261→  pointsExtractActions.reset();
   262→  pointsProductsActions.reset();
   263→  rescueActions.reset();
   264→  marketplaceActions.reset();
   265→}
   266→```
   267→
   268→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   269→
   270→## 6. Extract Store Extended with Expiring Filters
   271→
   272→Added to extract.model.ts:
   273→```typescript
   274→export interface ExpiringFilters {
   275→  period?: number;
   276→}
   277→
   278→export interface PointsExtractFiltersState {
   279→  appliedTransactionsFilters?: AppliedFilters;
   280→  appliedExpiringFilters?: ExpiringFilters;  // NEW
   281→}
   282→```
   283→
   284→Added actions to extract.store.ts:
   285→  - setExpiringFilters: (filters: ExpiringFilters) => void
   286→  - clearExpiringFilters: () => void
   287→
   288→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   289→
   290→## 7. Parameter Store Extended with Points Quantity
   291→
   292→Added to parameter.model.ts:
   293→```typescript
   294→export interface ParameterPointsProgramState {
   295→  // ... existing fields
   296→  pointsQuantity: number;      // NEW (default: 0)
   297→  conversionFactor: number;    // NEW (default: 0.1)
   298→}
   299→```
   300→
   301→Added actions to parameter.store.ts:
   302→  - setPointsQuantity: (quantity: number) => void
   303→  - setConversionFactor: (factor: number) => void
   304→
   305→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   306→
   307→## 8. Test Infrastructure Issue
   308→
   309→ISSUE FOUND: Jest test environment has pre-existing configuration problems.
   310→Error: "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'testEnvironmentOptions')"
   311→
   312→ROOT CAUSE: jest-environment-jsdom package issue, NOT related to test code.
   313→This affects ALL tests in the project, not just new ones.
   314→
   315→CREATED: __tests__/setup.js with basic mocks for:
   316→  - ~/store
   317→  - @semparar/mf-app-user
   318→  - zustand/shallow
   319→
   320→Test files created follow correct pattern from airlineMiles/store.test.ts.
   321→
   322→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   323→
   324→## 9. Pre-existing TypeScript Errors
   325→
   326→The project has 76 pre-existing TypeScript errors in 28 files.
   327→These are NOT related to the migration work.
   328→
   329→Key pre-existing issues:
   330→  - src/redux/ducks/points.ts - Missing type imports (PointsTransaction, ProcessedExtractData)
   331→  - src/services/repository/extract.repository.ts - TransactionType mismatch
   332→  - Various screen components with design system type mismatches
   333→
   334→NEW CODE HAS ZERO TYPESCRIPT ERRORS.
   335→
   336→================================================================================
   337→FINAL STATE OF CLEANUP FUNCTION
   338→================================================================================
   339→
   340→src/services/utils/cleanup.ts:
   341→```typescript
   342→import { useRootStore } from '~/store';
   343→
   344→export function clearRewardsProgramStore() {
   345→  const {
   346→    parameterPointsProgramActions,
   347→    rewardPermissionActions,
   348→    airlineMilesActions,
   349→    pointsExtractActions,
   350→    pointsProductsActions,
   351→    rescueActions,
   352→    marketplaceActions,
   353→  } = useRootStore.getState();
   354→
   355→  parameterPointsProgramActions.reset();
   356→  rewardPermissionActions.reset();
   357→  airlineMilesActions.reset();
   358→  pointsExtractActions.reset();
   359→  pointsProductsActions.reset();
   360→  rescueActions.reset();
   361→  marketplaceActions.reset();
   362→}
   363→```
   364→
   365→================================================================================
   366→VERIFICATION COMMANDS
   367→================================================================================
   368→
   369→npm run typecheck   # TypeScript validation (new files have no errors)
   370→npm test            # Tests have pre-existing infrastructure issues
   371→
   372→================================================================================
   373→REMAINING WORK (Future Sessions)
   374→================================================================================
   375→
   376→## Screen Integration (Not part of this migration)
   377→- Update screens to use Zustand stores instead of Redux
   378→- Remove mapStateToProps/mapDispatchToProps
   379→- Replace with useRootStore hooks
   380→
   381→## Cleanup Legacy Redux (After screen migration)
   382→- Remove unused Redux ducks after all screens migrated
   383→- Remove Redux-Saga watchers for migrated domains
   384→
   385→## Fix Pre-existing Issues (Optional)
   386→- Fix 76 TypeScript errors in 28 files
   387→- Fix Jest environment configuration
   388→
   389→================================================================================
   390→SESSION HISTORY
   391→================================================================================
   392→
   393→## 2026-01-26 Session 1: Complete Migration Planning
   394→- Studied STYLE_GUIDE.md and src/services/ structure
   395→- Verified all 4 existing Zustand domains are complete
   396→- Found bug in cleanup.ts (missing pointsExtractActions.reset)
   397→- Analyzed Redux ducks: points.ts, rescue.ts, getMarketplace.ts
   398→- Identified 3 domains to migrate: products, rescue, marketplace
   399→- Created comprehensive task list with 41 subtasks
   400→- No code changes made - planning only
   401→
   402→## 2026-01-26 Session 2: Complete Implementation
   403→- Executed all 8 phases of migration
   404→- Created 17 new files
   405→- Modified 12 existing files
   406→- Fixed participantId async handling issue
   407→- Discovered pre-existing TypeScript errors (not related to migration)
   408→- Discovered pre-existing Jest configuration issue
   409→- All new code has ZERO TypeScript errors
   410→- Documented all learnings for future reference
   411→
   412→Key Decisions Made:
   413→  1. Follow existing Zustand patterns exactly
   414→  2. Convert isLoading/isError booleans to Status enum
   415→  3. Keep all logic identical - structure change only
   416→  4. Handle callbacks in service layer (not store)
   417→  5. Move participantId retrieval to service layer
   418→  6. ALL slices must be cleared in cleanup function
   419→
   420→================================================================================
   421→API ENDPOINTS REFERENCE
   422→================================================================================
   423→
   424→Products: GET /fintech/v1/rewardProgram/productslist
   425→  Headers: { participantId }
   426→  Response: { dados: PointsProduct[], status: { codigoRetorno, mensagem } }
   427→
   428→Rescue: POST /fintech/v1/rewardProgram/rescue
   429→  Body: { value, campaignId?, productCode?, description, participantId }
   430→  Response: { status: { message }, data?: { status } }
   431→
   432→Marketplace: GET /marketplace/v1/marketplace/getToken?{params}
   433→  Response: { dados: { redirectURL }, status: { codigoRetorno, mensagem } }
   434→
   435→================================================================================

================================================================================
KEY LEARNINGS & IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS
================================================================================

## 1. participantId Handling Pattern

LEARNING: The `getParticipantIdFromToken()` function returns Promise<string | undefined>,
not a synchronous value. This caused TypeScript errors initially.

SOLUTION: Move participantId retrieval to the SERVICE layer, not the API layer.
The API layer should accept participantId as a parameter.

CORRECT PATTERN:
```typescript
// In service (products.service.ts):
const participantId = await getParticipantIdFromToken();
const response = await pointsProductsApi.getProducts(participantId);

// In API (products.api.ts):
async getProducts(participantId?: string): Promise<PointsProductsApiResponse> {
  const response = await api.get<PointsProductsApiResponse>(
    `/fintech/v1/rewardProgram/productslist`,
    { headers: { ...(participantId && { participantId }) } }
  );
  return response.data;
}
```

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 2. Status Enum Pattern vs Boolean Flags

LEARNING: Redux uses isLoading/isError booleans. Zustand in this project uses Status enums.

Pattern conversion:
  - isLoading: true  -> status: Status.LOADING
  - isError: true    -> status: Status.ERROR
  - success state    -> status: Status.SUCCESS
  - initial state    -> status: Status.IDLE

Benefits:
  - Single source of truth for state
  - Cleaner conditional logic
  - Easier to add new states (e.g., REFRESHING)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 3. Callback Handling in Rescue Domain

LEARNING: Redux rescue action accepts callbacks (onSuccess, onFailed) in the action payload.
These callbacks were handled by Redux-Saga middleware.

SOLUTION: In Zustand, handle callbacks in the SERVICE layer, not the store.

```typescript
// rescue.service.ts
export async function performRescue(
  payload: RescuePayload,
  callbacks?: RescueCallbacks
): Promise<void> {
  try {
    rescueActions.setStatus(RescueStatus.LOADING);
    const response = await rescueApi.rescue(payload);

    if (response?.data?.status === 'OK') {
      rescueActions.setStatus(RescueStatus.SUCCESS);
      callbacks?.onSuccess?.(response.status);  // Callback here
    } else {
      rescueActions.setStatus(RescueStatus.ERROR);
      callbacks?.onFailed?.(response?.status);   // Callback here
    }
  } catch (error) {
    rescueActions.setStatus(RescueStatus.ERROR);
    callbacks?.onFailed?.();
  }
}
```

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 4. Store Slice Composition

LEARNING: All slices must be added to:
  1. RewardsProgramRootStore type (intersection type)
  2. setRewardsProgramSlices function
  3. clearRewardsProgramStore cleanup function

Final slices.ts structure:
```typescript
export type RewardsProgramRootStore =
  ParameterPointsProgramSlice &
  PermissionSlice &
  AirlineMilesSlice &
  PointsExtractSlice &
  PointsProductsSlice &
  RescueSlice &
  MarketplaceSlice;
```

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 5. Cleanup Function - ALL Slices Must Be Cleared

CRITICAL: The clearRewardsProgramStore() function must reset ALL slices.
Missing any slice will cause state persistence issues across sessions.

Final cleanup.ts:
```typescript
export function clearRewardsProgramStore() {
  const {
    parameterPointsProgramActions,
    rewardPermissionActions,
    airlineMilesActions,
    pointsExtractActions,
    pointsProductsActions,
    rescueActions,
    marketplaceActions,
  } = useRootStore.getState();

  parameterPointsProgramActions.reset();
  rewardPermissionActions.reset();
  airlineMilesActions.reset();
  pointsExtractActions.reset();
  pointsProductsActions.reset();
  rescueActions.reset();
  marketplaceActions.reset();
}
```

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 6. Extract Store Extended with Expiring Filters

Added to extract.model.ts:
```typescript
export interface ExpiringFilters {
  period?: number;
}

export interface PointsExtractFiltersState {
  appliedTransactionsFilters?: AppliedFilters;
  appliedExpiringFilters?: ExpiringFilters;  // NEW
}
```

Added actions to extract.store.ts:
  - setExpiringFilters: (filters: ExpiringFilters) => void
  - clearExpiringFilters: () => void

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 7. Parameter Store Extended with Points Quantity

Added to parameter.model.ts:
```typescript
export interface ParameterPointsProgramState {
  // ... existing fields
  pointsQuantity: number;      // NEW (default: 0)
  conversionFactor: number;    // NEW (default: 0.1)
}
```

Added actions to parameter.store.ts:
  - setPointsQuantity: (quantity: number) => void
  - setConversionFactor: (factor: number) => void

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 8. Test Infrastructure Issue

ISSUE FOUND: Jest test environment has pre-existing configuration problems.
Error: "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'testEnvironmentOptions')"

ROOT CAUSE: jest-environment-jsdom package issue, NOT related to test code.
This affects ALL tests in the project, not just new ones.

CREATED: __tests__/setup.js with basic mocks for:
  - ~/store
  - @semparar/mf-app-user
  - zustand/shallow

Test files created follow correct pattern from airlineMiles/store.test.ts.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 9. Pre-existing TypeScript Errors

The project has 76 pre-existing TypeScript errors in 28 files.
These are NOT related to the migration work.

Key pre-existing issues:
  - src/redux/ducks/points.ts - Missing type imports (PointsTransaction, ProcessedExtractData)
  - src/services/repository/extract.repository.ts - TransactionType mismatch
  - Various screen components with design system type mismatches

NEW CODE HAS ZERO TYPESCRIPT ERRORS.

================================================================================
FINAL STATE OF CLEANUP FUNCTION
================================================================================

src/services/utils/cleanup.ts:
```typescript
import { useRootStore } from '~/store';

export function clearRewardsProgramStore() {
  const {
    parameterPointsProgramActions,
    rewardPermissionActions,
    airlineMilesActions,
    pointsExtractActions,
    pointsProductsActions,
    rescueActions,
    marketplaceActions,
  } = useRootStore.getState();

  parameterPointsProgramActions.reset();
  rewardPermissionActions.reset();
  airlineMilesActions.reset();
  pointsExtractActions.reset();
  pointsProductsActions.reset();
  rescueActions.reset();
  marketplaceActions.reset();
}
```

================================================================================
VERIFICATION COMMANDS
================================================================================

npm run typecheck   # TypeScript validation (new files have no errors)
npm test            # Tests have pre-existing infrastructure issues

================================================================================
REMAINING WORK (Future Sessions)
================================================================================

## Screen Integration (Not part of this migration)
- Update screens to use Zustand stores instead of Redux
- Remove mapStateToProps/mapDispatchToProps
- Replace with useRootStore hooks

## Cleanup Legacy Redux (After screen migration)
- Remove unused Redux ducks after all screens migrated
- Remove Redux-Saga watchers for migrated domains

## Fix Pre-existing Issues (Optional)
- Fix 76 TypeScript errors in 28 files
- Fix Jest environment configuration

================================================================================
SESSION HISTORY
================================================================================

## 2026-01-26 Session 1: Complete Migration Planning
- Studied STYLE_GUIDE.md and src/services/ structure
- Verified all 4 existing Zustand domains are complete
- Found bug in cleanup.ts (missing pointsExtractActions.reset)
- Analyzed Redux ducks: points.ts, rescue.ts, getMarketplace.ts
- Identified 3 domains to migrate: products, rescue, marketplace
- Created comprehensive task list with 41 subtasks
- No code changes made - planning only

## 2026-01-26 Session 2: Complete Implementation
- Executed all 8 phases of migration
- Created 17 new files
- Modified 12 existing files
- Fixed participantId async handling issue
- Discovered pre-existing TypeScript errors (not related to migration)
- Discovered pre-existing Jest configuration issue
- All new code has ZERO TypeScript errors
- Documented all learnings for future reference

Key Decisions Made:
  1. Follow existing Zustand patterns exactly
  2. Convert isLoading/isError booleans to Status enum
  3. Keep all logic identical - structure change only
  4. Handle callbacks in service layer (not store)
  5. Move participantId retrieval to service layer
  6. ALL slices must be cleared in cleanup function

================================================================================
API ENDPOINTS REFERENCE
================================================================================

Products: GET /fintech/v1/rewardProgram/productslist
  Headers: { participantId }
  Response: { dados: PointsProduct[], status: { codigoRetorno, mensagem } }

Rescue: POST /fintech/v1/rewardProgram/rescue
  Body: { value, campaignId?, productCode?, description, participantId }
  Response: { status: { message }, data?: { status } }

Marketplace: GET /marketplace/v1/marketplace/getToken?{params}
  Response: { dados: { redirectURL }, status: { codigoRetorno, mensagem } }

================================================================================

# mf-app-fintech-rewardprogram

React Native microfrontend for Reward Program. Consumed by SuperApp.

## Quick Start

1. Read `progress.txt` for current tasks and detailed implementation notes
2. Read `STYLE_GUIDE.md` for Zustand + Hook Component patterns
3. Run `npm run typecheck` to verify TypeScript

## Architecture

| Aspect | Technology |
|--------|------------|
| Type | React Native microfrontend (exports raw TypeScript) |
| State | Zustand (new domains) + Redux (legacy, being migrated) |
| Pattern | Hook Component (`index.ts` logic + `view.tsx` render) |

## Project Structure

```
src/
├── services/          # Domain logic (Zustand - SOLID architecture)
│   ├── api/           # HTTP clients
│   ├── models/        # Types, interfaces, enums
│   ├── repository/    # Data mapping
│   ├── services/      # Business logic
│   ├── store/         # Zustand slices
│   ├── hooks/         # Service hooks
│   └── utils/         # Helpers (cleanup, participantId)
├── screens/           # 10 screens (Hook Component pattern)
├── atomic/            # UI components (atoms, organisms, templates)
├── redux/             # Legacy state (being migrated to Zustand)
│   └── ducks/         # points.ts, rescue.ts, getMarketplace.ts
├── routes/            # Navigation
├── hooks/             # General hooks
└── index.ts           # Exports
```

## Zustand Domains

| Domain | Status | Layers |
|--------|--------|--------|
| airlineMiles | Complete | api, model, service, store, hook |
| extract | Complete | api, model, repository, service, store |
| parameter | Complete | api, model, repository, service, store |
| permission | Complete | api, model, repository, service, store, hook |
| products | Pending | needs all layers |
| rescue | Pending | needs all layers |
| marketplace | Pending | needs all layers |

## The `~` Alias (Critical)

All `~/` imports resolve to **SuperApp's code** at runtime:
- `~/store` → SuperApp's Zustand store (MANDATORY for store imports)
- `~/redux/*` → Legacy Redux
- `~/routes/*` → Navigation

**Tests only**: `~` maps to `./src/` via babel module-resolver.

## Code Rules

1. Store imports **MUST** use `~/store` (never relative paths)
2. Screens **MUST** follow Hook Component pattern
3. **NO LOGIC CHANGES** during migration - structure only
4. Verify with `npm run typecheck`
5. Never `git push`, only commit

## Commands

| Command | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `npm run typecheck` | TypeScript validation |
| `npm run sync-typings` | Generate types from SuperApp |
| `yarn sync:yalc` | Push to SuperApp for testing |
| `npm test` | Run tests |

## Exports (src/index.ts)

```typescript
export { RewardsProgramRoutes } from './routes';
export { type RewardsProgramRootStore } from './services';
export { setRewardsProgramSlices } from './services/store/slices';
export { clearRewardsProgramStore } from './services/utils';
```

## Current Work

See `progress.txt` for:
- Detailed migration tasks (41 subtasks)
- Redux state shapes to migrate
- Zustand patterns to follow
- Test patterns
- Complete code snippets

## Documentation

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `CLAUDE.md` | This file - quick reference |
| `progress.txt` | Detailed tasks, code snippets, persistent memory |
| `STYLE_GUIDE.md` | Zustand + Hook Component patterns (root folder) |

1→# Frontend Style Guide — Sem Parar (Zustand-only + Hook Component Pattern)
     2→
     3→Welcome to the **Sem Parar Frontend Style Guide**.  
     4→This version consolidates: **Zustand as the only state manager**, **Hook Component pattern** for Screens and Atomic components, **services architecture (SOLID)**, and an **cache pattern**.
     5→
     6→> Sit down, have a cup of coffee and read this calmly. ☕
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## :pushpin: Table of Contents
    11→- [Introduction](#introduction)
    12→- [Components & Screens — Hook Component Pattern](#components--screens--hook-component-pattern)
    13→- [Atomic Components — Hook Component Pattern](#atomic-components--hook-component-pattern)
    14→- [Project Organization](#project-organization)
    15→- [Services Architecture](#services-architecture)
    16→- [State Management (Zustand-only)](#state-management-zustand-only)
    17→- [Routes Naming](#routes-naming)
    18→- [Folder Naming Rules](#folder-naming-rules)
    19→- [Code Standards](#code-standards)
    20→- [Styles Patterns](#styles-patterns)
    21→- [Cache Pattern](#cache-pattern)
    22→- [Checklist](#checklist)
    23→
    24→---
    25→
    26→## Introduction
    27→
    28→The goal of this guide is to ensure **consistency, testability and scalability** across our frontend projects.  
    29→
    30→Key decisions:
    31→- **Zustand** is the **exclusive** global/shared state manager.
    32→- UI follows the **Hook Component** pattern (logic hook + dumb view).
    33→- Domain logic lives in **`/services`** with **SOLID** layering.
    34→- Cache is **typed and reusable**.
    35→
    36→---
    37→
    38→## Components & Screens — Hook Component Pattern
    39→
    40→Each screen is split into a logic hook and a render-only component.
    41→
    42→```
    43→screens/Home/
    44→├── index.ts        # logic: export useHomeScreen()
    45→├── view.tsx        # render: export default HomeScreen
    46→└── Models/
    47→    └── index.d.ts    # types (Props, State, Handlers, Return)
    48→```
    49→
    50→**Models/index.d.ts**
    51→```ts
    52→export interface HomeParams {
    53→  welcome?: string;
    54→}
    55→export interface HomeState {
    56→  greeting: string;
    57→  isLoading: boolean;
    58→}
    59→export interface HomeHandlers {
    60→  onPressCTA: () => void;
    61→}
    62→export type UseHomeReturn = HomeState & HomeHandlers;
    63→```
    64→
    65→**index.ts (logic hook)**
    66→```ts
    67→import { useCallback, useMemo, useState, useEffect } from "react";
    68→import type { HomeParams, UseHomeReturn } from "./Models";
    69→import { useUserStore } from "@/services/user/store"; // Zustand slice example
    70→
    71→export function useHomeScreen(params?: HomeParams): UseHomeReturn {
    72→  const name = useUserStore((s) => s.name);
    73→  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(true);
    74→
    75→  useEffect(() => {
    76→    const t = setTimeout(() => setIsLoading(false), 300);
    77→    return () => clearTimeout(t);
    78→  }, []);
    79→
    80→  const greeting = useMemo(
    81→    () => params?.welcome ?? `Hello, ${name || "guest"}!`,
    82→    [params?.welcome, name]
    83→  );
    84→
    85→  const onPressCTA = useCallback(() => {
    86→    // Call services, analytics, navigation, etc.
    87→  }, []);
    88→
    89→  return { greeting, isLoading, onPressCTA };
    90→}
    91→```
    92→
    93→**view.tsx (render)**
    94→```tsx
    95→import React from "react";
    96→import { View } from "react-native";
    97→import { Text, Button } from "@semparar/design-system-app";
    98→import { useHomeScreen } from "./index";
    99→import type { HomeParams } from "./Models";
   100→
   101→type Props = HomeParams;
   102→
   103→export default function HomeScreen(props: Props) {
   104→  const { greeting, isLoading, onPressCTA } = useHomeScreen(props);
   105→
   106→  if (isLoading) {
   107→    return (
   108→      <View>
   109→        <Text>Loading...</Text>
   110→      </View>
   111→    );
   112→  }
   113→
   114→  return (
   115→    <View>
   116→      <Text>{greeting}</Text>
   117→      <Button onPress={onPressCTA} text="Continue" />
   118→    </View>
   119→  );
   120→}
   121→```
   122→
   123→---
   124→
   125→## Atomic Components — Hook Component Pattern
   126→
   127→```
   128→atomic/atoms/Button/
   129→├── index.ts        # logic: export useButton()
   130→├── view.tsx        # render: export default Button
   131→└── Models/
   132→    └── index.d.ts
   133→```
   134→
   135→**Models/index.d.ts**
   136→```ts
   137→export interface ButtonProps {
   138→  text: string;
   139→  disabled?: boolean;
   140→  onPress?: () => void;
   141→}
   142→export interface ButtonState {
   143→  pressed: boolean;
   144→}
   145→export interface ButtonHandlers {
   146→  handlePress: () => void;
   147→}
   148→export type UseButtonReturn = ButtonState & ButtonHandlers;
   149→```
   150→
   151→**index.ts (logic hook)**
   152→```ts
   153→import { useCallback, useState } from "react";
   154→import type { ButtonProps, UseButtonReturn } from "./Models";
   155→
   156→export function useButton(props: ButtonProps): UseButtonReturn {
   157→  const [pressed, setPressed] = useState(false);
   158→
   159→  const handlePress = useCallback(() => {
   160→    setPressed(true);
   161→    props.onPress?.();
   162→    setTimeout(() => setPressed(false), 150);
   163→  }, [props.onPress]);
   164→
   165→  return { pressed, handlePress };
   166→}
   167→```
   168→
   169→**view.tsx (render)**
   170→```tsx
   171→import React from "react";
   172→import { Pressable, View } from "react-native";
   173→import { Text } from "@semparar/design-system-app";
   174→import { useButton } from "./index";
   175→import type { ButtonProps } from "./Models";
   176→
   177→export default function Button(props: ButtonProps) {
   178→  const { pressed, handlePress } = useButton(props);
   179→
   180→  return (
   181→    <Pressable disabled={props.disabled} onPress={handlePress}>
   182→      <View>
   183→        <Text>{props.text}{pressed ? " •" : ""}</Text>
   184→      </View>
   185→    </Pressable>
   186→  );
   187→}
   188→```
   189→
   190→---
   191→
   192→## Project Organization
   193→
   194→```
   195→src/
   196→├── @types/            # Global TS types
   197→├── assets/            # Images, fonts, icons
   198→├── atomic/            # UI components (Hook Component pattern)
   199→│   ├── atoms/
   200→│   ├── molecules/
   201→│   └── organisms/
   202→├── modules/           # Microfrontends shared and native modules
   203→├── screens/           # Page-level (Hook Component pattern)
   204→├── services/          # Domain logic (SOLID layout)
   205→│   ├── api/           # HTTP layer (Axios/Fetch)
   206→│   ├── class/         # Class with logical requests
   207→│   ├── hooks/         # Specific service hooks
   208→│   ├── repository/    # Data abstraction & mapping
   209→│   ├── services/      # Business orchestration
   210→│   ├── storage/       # Storage orchestration with cache or not
   211→│   ├── store/         # Zustand slices (only)
   212→│   ├── models/        # Domain types & contracts
   213→│   ├── utils/         # Reusable service helps
   214→│   └── index.ts       # Public exports
   215→├── hooks/             # Cross-cutting React hooks
   216→├── routes/            # Navigation config
   217→├── settings/          # Env & global config (axios, etc.)
   218→└── utils/             # General-purpose helpers
   219→```
   220→
   221→> UI **never** calls API directly. All effects & side effects live in the **logic hooks** (which call `/services`).
   222→
   223→---
   224→
   225→## Services Architecture
   226→
   227→**Flow:** `UI → services → repository → api` and `UI → services → store (Zustand)`
   228→
   229→- **api/**: request clients; no domain logic.  
   230→- **class/**: class with logical requests.  
   231→- **hooks/**: specific service hooks.
   232→- **repository/**: maps transport to domain, aggregates sources.  
   233→- **services/**: orchestration and business rules.  
   234→- **storage/**: storage orchestration with cache or not.  
   235→- **store/**: Zustand slices (single source of app state).  
   236→- **models/**: types, DTOs, contracts.  
   237→- **utils/**: reusable service helpers.
   238→
   239→Principles:
   240→- Single responsibility per folder/file.
   241→- No circular dependencies.
   242→- Public APIs exported via `services/index.ts` per domain.
   243→
   244→---
   245→
   246→## State Management (Zustand-only)
   247→
   248→- All global/shared state is **Zustand**.  
   249→- Slices live under `/services/<domain>/store/`.  
   250→- Use `persist` (with `createJSONStorage`) only when needed.  
   251→- Keep setters pure; side effects go in hooks or services.
   252→
   253→**Slice example**
   254→```ts
   255→import type { StateCreator } from "zustand";
   256→import type { AuthActions, AuthState } from "../models/store";
   257→
   258→export type AuthSlice = {
   259→    auth: AuthState;
   260→    authActions: AuthActions
   261→};
   262→
   263→const initialAuth = {
   264→    user: null,
   265→    loading: false,
   266→    error: undefined,
   267→}
   268→
   269→export const createAuthSlice: StateCreator<AuthSlice, [], [], AuthSlice> = (set) => ({
   270→    auth: initialAuth,
   271→    authActions: {
   272→        setLoading: (v) => set((s) => ({ auth: { ...s.auth, isLoading: v } })),
   273→        setUser: (v) => set((s) => ({ auth: { ...s.auth, user: v } })),
   274→        setError: (v) => set((s) => ({ auth: { ...s.auth, error: v } })),
   275→        reset: () => set({ auth: initialAuth }),
   276→    }
   277→});
   278→```
   279→
   280→**Root Store**
   281→
   282→- Applies to ALL Sem Parar frontend apps — including SuperApp.
   283→
   284→```ts
   285→import { create } from "zustand";
   286→import { devtools, persist, createJSONStorage } from "zustand/middleware";
   287→import { immer } from "zustand/middleware/immer";
   288→
   289→import { createUserSlice, type UserSlice } from "@semparar/mf-app-user/src/newArch/user";
   290→import { createAuthSlice, type AuthSlice } from "@semparar/mf-app-yourmf";
   291→
   292→import storage from "~/redux/store/storage";
   293→
   294→/** Add your slice Type */
   295→export type RootStore = UserSlice & AuthSlice;
   296→
   297→export const useRootStore = create<RootStore>()(
   298→  devtools(
   299→    persist(
   300→      immer((...a) => ({
   301→        ...createUserSlice(...a),
   302→        ...createAuthSlice(...a), // Add your slice here
   303→      })),
   304→      {
   305→        name: "root-storage",
   306→        storage: createJSONStorage(() => storage),
   307→        partialize: (s) => ({
   308→          user: s.user,
   309→          auth: s.auth, // Add your store data
   310→        }),
   311→        merge: (persisted, current) => ({ ...current, ...(persisted as Partial<RootStore>) }),
   312→      }
   313→    ),
   314→    { name: "RootStore" }
   315→  )
   316→);
   317→```
   318→
   319→- For Use on Class.
   320→
   321→```ts
   322→class Auth {
   323→  private readonly setAuth;
   324→
   325→  constructor () {
   326→    this.setUser = useRootStore.getState().authActions.setUser;
   327→  }
   328→
   329→  function doSomething(data) {
   330→    this.setUser({ ...data });
   331→  }
   332→}
   333→```
   334→
   335→- For Use on Component/Screen.
   336→- UI never imports the store directly — uses selectors
   337→
   338→```ts
   339→const loading = useRootStore((s) => s.user.isLoading);
   340→const token = useRootStore((s) => s.auth.token);
   341→```
   342→
   343→---
   344→
   345→## Routes Naming
   346→
   347→Standardize as **`Acronym + Route`**:  
   348→- ✅ `SPPayment` (Sem Parar Payment)  
   349→- ✅ `FTHPayment` (Fintech Payment)
   350→
   351→---
   352→
   353→## Folder Naming Rules
   354→
   355→| Folder                              | Convention   | Examples                    |
   356→|-------------------------------------|--------------|-----------------------------|
   357→| `atomic`, `screens`, `routes`       | `PascalCase` | `HomeScreen`, `CarDetails`  |
   358→| `assets`, `hooks`, `services`, etc. | `camelCase`  | `useAuth`, `authService`    |
   359→
   360→---
   361→
   362→## Code Standards
   363→
   364→**Language**: English only.  
   365→**Naming**: `camelCase` (vars/functions), `PascalCase` (components/classes), `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` (constants).
   366→
   367→**Explanatory variables**  
   368→```js
   369→const onlyNumbersRegex = /^\d+$/;
   370→const getNumberValidation = (message) => (value) => !onlyNumbersRegex.test(value) && message;
   371→const isNumber = getNumberValidation("error message");
   372→isNumber(1);
   373→```
   374→
   375→**Semantic constants**  
   376→```js
   377→const DAY_IN_MILLISECONDS = 86400000;
   378→setTimeout(doSomething, DAY_IN_MILLISECONDS);
   379→```
   380→
   381→**Library workarounds with link**  
   382→```js
   383→// Refer to issue https://github.com/ngokevin/react-file-reader-input/issues/11#issuecomment-363484861
   384→// eslint-disable-next-line no-param-reassign
   385→event.target.value = "";
   386→```
   387→
   388→**JSDoc**  
   389→```js
   390→/**
   391→ * Truncates an event name to the max length.
   392→ * @param {*} eventName
   393→ * @param {*} maxLength
   394→ */
   395→```
   396→
   397→**Hooks: multi-line deps & IIFE**  
   398→```ts
   399→useEffect(() => {
   400→  (async () => {
   401→    await CustomMath.randomSum();
   402→    setSum(42);
   403→  })();
   404→}, [
   405→  setSum,
   406→]);
   407→```
   408→
   409→**Extract conditions**  
   410→```ts
   411→const hasSelectedOffer = !!(selectedProduct || selectedLeasingOffer);
   412→if (!hasSelectedOffer && !individualOffer) return;
   413→```
   414→
   415→**Optional chaining**  
   416→```ts
   417→const answers = formikForm?.values?.answers;
   418→```
   419→
   420→**JSX indentation**  
   421→```tsx
   422→{
   423→  !isEnd && (
   424→    <Button text="CTA" onPress={handleNavigate} />
   425→  )
   426→}
   427→```
   428→
   429→**Early returns**  
   430→```ts
   431→if (!data) return null;
   432→return <View />;
   433→```
   434→
   435→**Nullish coalescing**  
   436→```ts
   437→return data ?? 0;
   438→```
   439→---
   440→
   441→## Styles Patterns
   442→
   443→- Do **not** use `StyleSheet` or `styled-components`.  
   444→- Use **@semparar/design-system-app** for components, tokens, and theming.
   445→
   446→---
   447→
   448→## Cache Pattern
   449→
   450→Keep cache typed, serializable, and domain.
   451→
   452→```ts
   453→import storage from "~/redux/store/storage";
   454→
   455→export interface CachedData<T> {
   456→  timestamp: number;
   457→  data: T;
   458→}
   459→
   460→export interface StorageContract<T> {
   461→  get(): Promise<CachedData<T> | null>;
   462→  save(data: T): Promise<void>;
   463→  remove(): Promise<void>;
   464→}
   465→
   466→export class GenericStorage<T> implements StorageContract<T> {
   467→  constructor(private readonly key: string) {}
   468→
   469→  async get(): Promise<CachedData<T> | null> {
   470→    const json = await storage.getItem(this.key);
   471→    if (!json) return null;
   472→    return JSON.parse(json) as CachedData<T>;
   473→  }
   474→
   475→  async save(data: T): Promise<void> {
   476→    const payload: CachedData<T> = { timestamp: Date.now(), data };
   477→    await storage.setItem(this.key, JSON.stringify(payload));
   478→  }
   479→
   480→  async remove(): Promise<void> {
   481→    await storage.removeItem(this.key);
   482→  }
   483→}
   484→```
   485→
   486→**Guidelines**
   487→- One storage class per module/feature.
   488→- Types/interfaces live in `Models/` where appropriate.
   489→- Use `timestamp` for freshness/expiry strategies.
   490→- Never persist secrets/PII outside secure storage.
   491→- Keep payloads small and JSON-serializable.
   492→
   493→---
   494→
   495→## Checklist
   496→
   497→- [ ] Screens/Components follow **Hook Component pattern** (`index.ts` hook + `view.tsx` render)
   498→- [ ] Types live in **Models/**
   499→- [ ] Only **Zustand** for global/shared state under **/services/<domain>/store/**
   500→- [ ] UI never calls API directly (go through **/services**)
   501→- [ ] Code standards and styles patterns applied
   502→- [ ] Cache implemented via **GenericStorage** (when needed)
   503→- [ ] Routes and folder naming conventions followed
   504→
   505→---
   506→
   507→Happy shipping! 🚀

content.text:
## .claude Folder Analysis

I've studied the complete contents of the `.claude` folder. Here's a comprehensive summary:

### Files Structure
```
.claude/
├── CLAUDE.md        # Quick reference for the project
├── STYLE_GUIDE.md   # Zustand + Hook Component patterns
└── progress.txt     # Permanent memory (most critical)
```

---

### progress.txt — Key Insights

**Project Status: MIGRATION COMPLETED**

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Zustand Domains | 7 complete |
| Files Created | 17 |
| Files Modified | 12 |
| Pre-existing TS Errors | 76 (not related to migration) |

**Domains Migrated:**
| Domain | Layers |
|--------|--------|
| airlineMiles | api, model, service, store, hook |
| extract | api, model, repository, service, store |
| parameter | api, model, repository, service, store |
| permission | api, model, repository, service, store, hook |
| products | api, model, repository, service, store |
| rescue | api, model, service, store, hook |
| marketplace | api, model, service, store |

**Critical Learnings Documented:**
1. **participantId** → retrieve in SERVICE layer (async), pass to API layer
2. **Status enum** replaces `isLoading/isError` booleans
3. **Callbacks** → handled in service layer, not store
4. **Cleanup** → ALL 7 slices must be reset in `clearRewardsProgramStore()`
5. **Store imports** → MUST use `~/store` (SuperApp runtime)

**Remaining Work (Future Sessions):**
- Screen integration: replace Redux with Zustand hooks
- Remove legacy Redux ducks after full migration
- Fix 76 pre-existing TypeScript errors (optional)

---

### STYLE_GUIDE.md — Architecture Patterns

**Hook Component Pattern:**
```
screens/Home/
├── index.ts     # logic hook: useHomeScreen()
├── view.tsx     # render: HomeScreen
└── Models/      # types
```

**Services Flow:**
```
UI → services → repository → api
UI → services → store (Zustand)
```

**Zustand Slice Structure:**
- State + Actions pattern
- Slices under `/services/<domain>/store/`
- Root store composition with type intersection

---

This documentation is well-maintained and serves as effective project memory. The migration appears complete—next phase would be integrating screens to use Zustand instead of Redux.

Now I have a complete picture of the migration failure. Let me summarize and update the progress.txt file with the tasks needed.

## Analysis Summary

### What the commit did:
1. Created Zustand stores for: products, rescue, marketplace
2. Created models, API, services for these domains
3. Updated slices.ts to include new slices
4. Updated cleanup.ts to reset all slices
5. Created tests for the new stores

### What the commit FAILED to do:

**1. Screens NOT migrated (still using Redux connect/mapStateToProps):**
- `src/screens/Points/index.tsx` → Uses `connect`, `PointsActions`, `AppReducers`
- `src/screens/PointsDetail/index.tsx` → Uses `connect`, `PointsActions`, `AppReducers`
- `src/screens/RewardProgramMarketplace/index.tsx` → Uses `connect`, `getMarketplaceActions`, `AppReducers`
- `src/screens/InvoiceCashbackOnboarding/index.tsx` → Uses `connect`, `PointsActions`, `AppReducers`
- `src/screens/InvoiceCashbackPoints/index.tsx` → Uses `connect`, `PointsActions`, `AppReducers`
- `src/screens/InvoiceCashbackSummary/index.tsx` → Uses `connect`, `PointsActions`, `rescueActions`, `AppReducers`

**2. Redux files NOT removed:**
- `src/redux/ducks/points.ts`
- `src/redux/ducks/rescue.ts`
- `src/redux/ducks/getMarketplace.ts`
- `src/redux/reducers/Models/index.ts`
- `src/redux/reducers/index.ts`
- `src/redux/sagas/points/models/index.ts`

**3. Hooks still using Redux:**


- `src/services/hooks/usePermission.ts` → Uses `useSelector` from react-redux for `customerReducer.nrc`

**4. Type imports still referencing Redux:**
- `src/hooks/custom/useCardBuilderWhereUseMyPoints/index.ts` and its data file pull `PointsProduct` from redux
- `src/routes/Models/index.ts` and `src/screens/Points/Models/index.ts` still import types from the old Redux structure
- `src/atomic/organisms/FiltersBottomSheet/index.tsx` → `FilterOption` remains in redux

**5. Dead code identified:**
- `src/services/hooks/useRescue.ts` is defined but never called—screens continue pulling from Redux instead

**6. Additional Redux dependencies:**
- `src/services/utils/participantId.ts` still depends on `~/redux/store/storage`

The core issue is the migration created Zustand but didn't complete the transition: screens weren't updated to use the new hooks, type definitions weren't relocated, Redux files weren't cleaned up, and import paths weren't adjusted accordingly.

# Reward Program - Redux to Zustand Migration

Last Updated: 2026-01-26 18:45
Status: INCOMPLETE - CORRECTION REQUIRED

================================================================================
MIGRATION FAILURE ANALYSIS
================================================================================

## Root Cause

The commit `5e947a35` (feat: migrate redux code) created Zustand infrastructure
but FAILED to complete the migration:

1. Zustand stores were created BUT screens still use Redux connect()
2. Types duplicated in both redux/ducks AND services/models
3. Redux files NOT removed
4. New Zustand hooks (useRescue) are UNUSED
5. Screens still import from redux/ducks

## Evidence (npm run knip)

```
Unused exports (1):
  useRescue function src/services/hooks/useRescue.ts:8:17  <- CREATED BUT NOT USED!

Unresolved imports (3 redux-related):
  ~/redux/store/createReducer  src/redux/ducks/getMarketplace.ts
  ~/redux/store/createReducer  src/redux/ducks/points.ts
  ~/redux/store/createReducer  src/redux/ducks/rescue.ts
```

================================================================================
CURRENT STATE (What exists)
================================================================================

## Zustand Infrastructure CREATED (but unused by screens):
| Domain      | API | Model | Repository | Service | Store | Hook |
|-------------|-----|-------|------------|---------|-------|------|
| products    | Yes | Yes   | Yes        | Yes     | Yes   | -    |
| rescue      | Yes | Yes   | -          | Yes     | Yes   | Yes* |
| marketplace | Yes | Yes   | -          | Yes     | Yes   | -    |

*useRescue exists but NO SCREEN uses it

## Redux Files STILL EXIST (should be deleted after migration):
- src/redux/ducks/points.ts
- src/redux/ducks/rescue.ts
- src/redux/ducks/getMarketplace.ts
- src/redux/reducers/Models/index.ts
- src/redux/reducers/index.ts
- src/redux/sagas/points/models/index.ts

## Screens STILL Using Redux (6 screens need migration):
| Screen                    | Redux Deps                                    |
|---------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|
| Points                    | connect, PointsActions, AppReducers           |
| PointsDetail              | connect, PointsActions, AppReducers           |
| RewardProgramMarketplace  | connect, getMarketplaceActions, AppReducers   |
| InvoiceCashbackOnboarding | connect, PointsActions, AppReducers           |
| InvoiceCashbackPoints     | connect, PointsActions, AppReducers           |
| InvoiceCashbackSummary    | connect, PointsActions, rescueActions         |

## Other Files Still Importing Redux:
- src/services/hooks/usePermission.ts → useSelector from react-redux
- src/hooks/custom/useCardBuilderWhereUseMyPoints/index.ts → PointsProduct
- src/hooks/custom/useCardBuilderWhereUseMyPoints/data/index.ts → PointsProduct
- src/routes/Models/index.ts → PointsTransaction, AppReducers, Dispatch
- src/screens/Points/Models/index.ts → PointsProduct, InitialState
- src/atomic/organisms/FiltersBottomSheet/index.tsx → FilterOption

================================================================================
CORRECTION TASKS
================================================================================

## Phase 1: Complete Type Migration [HIGH PRIORITY]

### 1.1 Move missing types to Zustand models
Files to update:
- [ ] src/services/models/products.model.ts
  - Add: PointsProduct interface (from redux/ducks/points.ts:42-56)
  - Add: FilterOption interface (from redux/ducks/points.ts:63-66)

- [ ] src/services/models/extract.model.ts
  - Add: PointsTransaction interface (if missing)
  - Add: ExpiringPoints interface
  - Add: PointsExtractData interface

### 1.2 Update type exports
- [ ] src/services/models/index.ts - export new types

## Phase 2: Create Missing Zustand Hooks [HIGH PRIORITY]

### 2.1 Create usePoints hook
File: src/services/hooks/usePoints.ts
```typescript
export function usePoints() {
  const pointsProducts = useRootStore(useShallow(state => state.pointsProducts));
  const parameterPointsProgram = useRootStore(useShallow(state => state.parameterPointsProgram));

  return {
    // Products
    products: pointsProducts.products,
    isLoadingProducts: pointsProducts.status === PointsProductsStatus.LOADING,
    isErrorProducts: pointsProducts.status === PointsProductsStatus.ERROR,

    // Balance (from parameter)
    points: parameterPointsProgram.pointsQuantity,
    isLoadingBalance: parameterPointsProgram.status === Status.LOADING,
    isErrorBalance: parameterPointsProgram.status === Status.ERROR,

    // Actions
    requestPointsProducts: () => getPointsProducts(),
    requestPointsBalance: () => getParameterPointsProgram(),
    clear: () => clearPointsProducts(),
  };
}
```

### 2.2 Create useMarketplace hook
File: src/services/hooks/useMarketplace.ts
```typescript
export function useMarketplace() {
  const marketplace = useRootStore(useShallow(state => state.marketplace));

  return {
    data: marketplace.data,
    isLoading: marketplace.status === MarketplaceStatus.LOADING,
    isError: marketplace.status === MarketplaceStatus.ERROR,
    requestGetMarketplace: (params) => getMarketplace(params),
    clear: () => clearMarketplace(),
  };
}
```

### 2.3 Update hooks/index.ts exports
- [ ] Add usePoints, useMarketplace exports

## Phase 3: Migrate Screens to Zustand [CRITICAL]

### 3.1 Points screen
File: src/screens/Points/index.tsx

- [ ] Add: import { usePoints, useRescue } from '../../services/hooks'
- [ ] Replace: pointsReducer with usePoints()
- [ ] Replace: rescueReducer with useRescue()

## Phase 4: Update Dependent Files

### 4.1 Fix usePermission hook
File: src/services/hooks/usePermission.ts
- [ ] Remove: useSelector from react-redux
- [ ] Solution: Get accountId from SuperApp's Zustand store or pass as parameter

### 4.2 Update type imports
Files to update:
- [ ] src/hooks/custom/useCardBuilderWhereUseMyPoints/index.ts
  - Change: import { PointsProduct } from '../../../services/models'

- [ ] src/hooks/custom/useCardBuilderWhereUseMyPoints/data/index.ts
  - Change: import { PointsProduct } from '../../../../services/models'

- [ ] src/routes/Models/index.ts
  - Change: Remove Redux imports, use Zustand types

- [ ] src/screens/Points/Models/index.ts
  - Change: import types from services/models

- [ ] src/atomic/organisms/FiltersBottomSheet/index.tsx
  - Change: import { FilterOption } from '../../../services/models'

## Phase 5: Cleanup Redux Files [AFTER SCREENS MIGRATED]

### 5.1 Delete Redux ducks
- [ ] Delete: src/redux/ducks/points.ts
- [ ] Delete: src/redux/ducks/rescue.ts

### 5.2 Delete Redux reducers

### 5.3 Delete Redux sagas

### 5.4 Clean up empty folders
- [ ] Delete src/redux/ folder if empty

## Phase 6: Verification

### 6.1 TypeScript check
- [ ] Run: npm run typecheck
- [ ] Verify: Zero errors in migrated files

### 6.2 Knip check
- [ ] Run: npm run knip
- [ ] Verify: No unused exports in services/hooks/
- [ ] Verify: No unresolved imports to redux/

### 6.3 Functional test
- [ ] Test: Points screen loads products
- [ ] Test: Marketplace screen loads redirect URL
- [ ] Test: InvoiceCashbackSummary rescue flow works
- [ ] Test: Navigation between screens works

================================================================================
MIGRATION PATTERN REFERENCE
================================================================================

## Before (Redux):
```typescript
// Screen with Redux
import { connect } from 'react-redux';
import { bindActionCreators, Dispatch } from 'redux';
import { PointsActions } from '../../redux/ducks/points';
import { AppReducers } from '../../redux/reducers/Models';

const Screen: FC<Props> = ({ pointsReducer, requestPointsProducts }) => {
  useEffect(() => {
    requestPointsProducts();
  }, []);

  return <View isLoading={pointsReducer.isLoadingProducts} />;
};

export const mapStateToProps = ({ pointsReducer }: AppReducers) => ({
  pointsReducer,
});

export const mapDispatchToProps = (dispatch: Dispatch) => {
  return bindActionCreators({ ...PointsActions }, dispatch);
};

export default connect(mapStateToProps, mapDispatchToProps)(Screen);
```

## After (Zustand):
```typescript
// Screen with Zustand
import { usePoints } from '../../services/hooks';

const Screen: FC<Props> = () => {
  const { products, isLoadingProducts, requestPointsProducts } = usePoints();

  useEffect(() => {
    requestPointsProducts();
  }, []);

  return <View isLoading={isLoadingProducts} />;
};

export default Screen;
```

-LEARNING: Redux rescue action accepts callbacks (onSuccess, onFailed) in the action payload.

src/services/hooks/index.ts

================================================================================
KEY LEARNINGS & IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS
================================================================================

## 1. participantId Handling Pattern

LEARNING: The `getParticipantIdFromToken()` function returns Promise<string | undefined>,
not a synchronous value. This caused TypeScript errors initially.

SOLUTION: Move participantId retrieval to the SERVICE layer, not the API layer.
The API layer should accept participantId as a parameter.

CORRECT PATTERN:
```typescript
// In service (products.service.ts):
const participantId = await getParticipantIdFromToken();
const response = await pointsProductsApi.getProducts(participantId);

// In API (products.api.ts):
async getProducts(participantId?: string): Promise<PointsProductsApiResponse> {
  const response = await api.get<PointsProductsApiResponse>(
    `/fintech/v1/rewardProgram/productslist`,
    { headers: { ...(participantId && { participantId }) } }
  );
  return response.data;
}
```

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 2. Status Enum Pattern vs Boolean Flags

LEARNING: Redux uses isLoading/isError booleans. Zustand in this project uses Status enums.

Pattern conversion:
  - isLoading: true  -> status: Status.LOADING
  - isError: true    -> status: Status.ERROR
  - success state    -> status: Status.SUCCESS
  - initial state    -> status: Status.IDLE

Benefits:
  - Single source of truth for state
  - Cleaner conditional logic
  - Easier to add new states (e.g., REFRESHING)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 3. Callback Handling in Rescue Domain

LEARNING: Redux rescue action accepts callbacks (onSuccess, onFailed) in the action payload.
These callbacks were handled by Redux-Saga middleware.

SOLUTION: In Zustand, handle callbacks in the SERVICE layer, not the store.

```typescript
// rescue.service.ts
export async function performRescue(
  payload: RescuePayload,
  callbacks?: RescueCallbacks
): Promise<void> {
  try {
    rescueActions.setStatus(RescueStatus.LOADING);
    const response = await rescueApi.rescue(payload);

    if (response?.data?.status === 'OK') {
      rescueActions.setStatus(RescueStatus.SUCCESS);
      callbacks?.onSuccess?.(response.status);  // Callback here
    } else {
      rescueActions.setStatus(RescueStatus.ERROR);
      callbacks?.onFailed?.(response?.status);   // Callback here
    }
  } catch (error) {
    rescueActions.setStatus(RescueStatus.ERROR);
    callbacks?.onFailed?.();
  }
}
```

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 4. Store Slice Composition

LEARNING: All slices must be added to:
  1. RewardsProgramRootStore type (intersection type)
  2. setRewardsProgramSlices function
  3. clearRewardsProgramStore cleanup function

Final slices.ts structure:
```typescript
export type RewardsProgramRootStore =
  ParameterPointsProgramSlice &
  PermissionSlice &
  AirlineMilesSlice &
  PointsExtractSlice &
  PointsProductsSlice &
  RescueSlice &
  MarketplaceSlice;
```

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 5. Cleanup Function - ALL Slices Must Be Cleared

CRITICAL: The clearRewardsProgramStore() function must reset ALL slices.
Missing any slice will cause state persistence issues across sessions.

Final cleanup.ts:
```typescript
export function clearRewardsProgramStore() {
  const {
    parameterPointsProgramActions,
    rewardPermissionActions,
    airlineMilesActions,
    pointsExtractActions,
    pointsProductsActions,
    rescueActions,
    marketplaceActions,
  } = useRootStore.getState();

  parameterPointsProgramActions.reset();
  rewardPermissionActions.reset();
  airlineMilesActions.reset();
  pointsExtractActions.reset();
  pointsProductsActions.reset();
  rescueActions.reset();
  marketplaceActions.reset();
}
```

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 6. Extract Store Extended with Expiring Filters

Added to extract.model.ts:
```typescript
export interface ExpiringFilters {
  period?: number;
}

export interface PointsExtractFiltersState {
  appliedTransactionsFilters?: AppliedFilters;
  appliedExpiringFilters?: ExpiringFilters;  // NEW
}
```

Added actions to extract.store.ts:
  - setExpiringFilters: (filters: ExpiringFilters) => void
  - clearExpiringFilters: () => void

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 7. Parameter Store Extended with Points Quantity

Added to parameter.model.ts:
```typescript
export interface ParameterPointsProgramState {
  // ... existing fields
  pointsQuantity: number;      // NEW (default: 0)
  conversionFactor: number;    // NEW (default: 0.1)
}
```

Added actions to parameter.store.ts:
  - setPointsQuantity: (quantity: number) => void
  - setConversionFactor: (factor: number) => void

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 8. Test Infrastructure Issue

ISSUE FOUND: Jest test environment has pre-existing configuration problems.
Error: "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'testEnvironmentOptions')"

ROOT CAUSE: jest-environment-jsdom package issue, NOT related to test code.
This affects ALL tests in the project, not just new ones.

CREATED: __tests__/setup.js with basic mocks for:
  - ~/store
  - @semparar/mf-app-user
  - zustand/shallow

Test files created follow correct pattern from airlineMiles/store.test.ts.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 9. Pre-existing TypeScript Errors

The project has 76 pre-existing TypeScript errors in 28 files.
These are NOT related to the migration work.

Key pre-existing issues:
  - src/redux/ducks/points.ts - Missing type imports (PointsTransaction, ProcessedExtractData)
  - src/services/repository/extract.repository.ts - TransactionType mismatch
  - Various screen components with design system type mismatches

NEW CODE HAS ZERO TYPESCRIPT ERRORS.

================================================================================
FINAL STATE OF CLEANUP FUNCTION
================================================================================

src/services/utils/cleanup.ts:
```typescript
import { useRootStore } from '~/store';

export function clearRewardsProgramStore() {
  const {
    parameterPointsProgramActions,
    rewardPermissionActions,
    airlineMilesActions,
    pointsExtractActions,
    pointsProductsActions,
    rescueActions,
    marketplaceActions,
  } = useRootStore.getState();

  parameterPointsProgramActions.reset();
  rewardPermissionActions.reset();
  airlineMilesActions.reset();
  pointsExtractActions.reset();
  pointsProductsActions.reset();
  rescueActions.reset();
  marketplaceActions.reset();
}
```

================================================================================
VERIFICATION COMMANDS
================================================================================

npm run typecheck   # TypeScript validation (new files have no errors)
npm test            # Tests have pre-existing infrastructure issues

================================================================================
REMAINING WORK (Future Sessions)
================================================================================

## Screen Integration (Not part of this migration)
- Update screens to use Zustand stores instead of Redux
- Remove mapStateToProps/mapDispatchToProps
- Replace with useRootStore hooks

## Cleanup Legacy Redux (After screen migration)
- Remove unused Redux ducks after all screens migrated
- Remove Redux-Saga watchers for migrated domains

## Fix Pre-existing Issues (Optional)
- Fix 76 TypeScript errors in 28 files
- Fix Jest environment configuration

================================================================================
SESSION HISTORY
================================================================================

## 2026-01-26 Session 1: Complete Migration Planning
- Studied STYLE_GUIDE.md and src/services/ structure
- Verified all 4 existing Zustand domains are complete
- Found bug in cleanup.ts (missing pointsExtractActions.reset)
- Analyzed Redux ducks: points.ts, rescue.ts, getMarketplace.ts
- Identified 3 domains to migrate: products, rescue, marketplace
- Created comprehensive task list with 41 subtasks
- No code changes made - planning only

## 2026-01-26 Session 2: Complete Implementation
- Executed all 8 phases of migration
- Created 17 new files
- Modified 12 existing files
- Fixed participantId async handling issue
- Discovered pre-existing TypeScript errors (not related to migration)
- Discovered pre-existing Jest configuration issue
- All new code has ZERO TypeScript errors
- Documented all learnings for future reference

Key Decisions Made:
  1. Follow existing Zustand patterns exactly
  2. Convert isLoading/isError booleans to Status enum
  3. Keep all logic identical - structure change only
  4. Handle callbacks in service layer (not store)
  5. Move participantId retrieval to service layer
  6. ALL slices must be cleared in cleanup function

================================================================================
API ENDPOINTS REFERENCE
================================================================================

Products: GET /fintech/v1/rewardProgram/productslist
  Headers: { participantId }
  Response: { dados: PointsProduct[], status: { codigoRetorno, mensagem } }

Rescue: POST /fintech/v1/rewardProgram/rescue
  Body: { value, campaignId?, productCode?, description, participantId }
  Response: { status: { message }, data?: { status } }

Marketplace: GET /marketplace/v1/marketplace/getToken?{params}
  Response: { dados: { redirectURL }, status: { codigoRetorno, mensagem } }

================================================================================

## 1. participantId Handling Pattern

SOLUTION: Move participantId retrieval to the SERVICE layer, not the API layer.

// In service (products.service.ts):
const participantId = await getParticipantIdFromToken();

async getProducts(participantId?: string): Promise<PointsProductsApiResponse> {

## 2. Status Enum Pattern vs Boolean Flags

LEARNING: Redux uses isLoading/isError booleans. Zustand in this project uses Status enums.

Pattern conversion:
  - isLoading: true  -> status: Status.LOADING
  - isError: true    -> status: Status.ERROR
  - success state    -> status: Status.SUCCESS
  - initial state    -> status: Status.IDLE

Benefits:
  - Single source of truth for state
  - Cleaner conditional logic
  - Easier to add new states (e.g., REFRESHING)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 3. Callback Handling in Rescue Domain

LEARNING: Redux rescue action accepts callbacks (onSuccess, onFailed) in the action payload.
These callbacks were handled by Redux-Saga middleware.

SOLUTION: In Zustand, handle callbacks in the SERVICE layer, not the store.

```typescript
// rescue.service.ts
export async function performRescue(
  payload: RescuePayload,
  callbacks?: RescueCallbacks
): Promise<void> {
  try {
    rescueActions.setStatus(RescueStatus.LOADING);
    const response = await rescueApi.rescue(payload);

    if (response?.data?.status === 'OK') {
      rescueActions.setStatus(RescueStatus.SUCCESS);
      callbacks?.onSuccess?.(response.status);  // Callback here
    } else {
      rescueActions.setStatus(RescueStatus.ERROR);
      callbacks?.onFailed?.(response?.status);   // Callback here

## 1. participantId Handling Pattern

LEARNING: The `getParticipantIdFromToken()` function returns Promise<string | undefined>,
not a synchronous value. This caused TypeScript errors initially.

SOLUTION: Move participantId retrieval to the SERVICE layer, not the API layer.
The API layer should accept participantId as a parameter.

CORRECT PATTERN:
```typescript
// In service (products.service.ts):
const participantId = await getParticipantIdFromToken();
const response = await pointsProductsApi.getProducts(participantId);

// In API (products.api.ts):
async getProducts(participantId?: string): Promise<PointsProductsApiResponse> {
  const response = await api.get<PointsProductsApiResponse>(
    `/fintech/v1/rewardProgram/productslist`,
    { headers: { ...(participantId && { participantId }) } }
  );
  return response.data;
}
```

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 2. Status Enum Pattern vs Boolean Flags

LEARNING: Redux uses isLoading/isError booleans. Zustand in this project uses Status enums.

Pattern conversion:
  - isLoading: true  -> status: Status.LOADING
  - isError: true    -> status: Status.ERROR
  - success state    -> status: Status.SUCCESS
  - initial state    -> status: Status.IDLE

Benefits:
  - Single source of truth for state
  - Cleaner conditional logic
  - Easier to add new states (e.g., REFRESHING)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 3. Callback Handling in Rescue Domain

LEARNING: Redux rescue action accepts callbacks (onSuccess, onFailed) in the action payload.
These callbacks were handled by Redux-Saga middleware.

SOLUTION: In Zustand, handle callbacks in the SERVICE layer, not the store.

```typescript
// rescue.service.ts
export async function performRescue(
  payload: RescuePayload,
  callbacks?: RescueCallbacks
): Promise<void> {
  try {
    rescueActions.setStatus(RescueStatus.LOADING);
    const response = await rescueApi.rescue(payload);

    if (response?.data?.status === 'OK') {
      rescueActions.setStatus(RescueStatus.SUCCESS);
      callbacks?.onSuccess?.(response.status);  // Callback here
    } else {
      rescueActions.setStatus(RescueStatus.ERROR);
      callbacks?.onFailed?.(response?.status);   // Callback here
    }
  } catch (error) {
    rescueActions.setStatus(RescueStatus.ERROR);
    callbacks?.onFailed?.();
  }
}
```

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  2. setRewardsProgramSlices function
  3. clearRewardsProgramStore cleanup function

   143→## 1. participantId Handling Pattern
   144→
   145→LEARNING: The `getParticipantIdFromToken()` function returns Promise<string | undefined>,
   146→not a synchronous value. This caused TypeScript errors initially.
   147→
   148→SOLUTION: Move participantId retrieval to the SERVICE layer, not the API layer.
   149→The API layer should accept participantId as a parameter.
   150→
   151→CORRECT PATTERN:

   162→  );
   163→  return response.data;
   164→}
   165→```
   166→
   167→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   168→
   169→## 2. Status Enum Pattern vs Boolean Flags
   170→
   171→LEARNING: Redux uses isLoading/isError booleans. Zustand in this project uses Status enums.
   172→
   173→Pattern conversion:
   174→  - isLoading: true  -> status: Status.LOADING
   175→  - isError: true    -> status: Status.ERROR
   176→  - success state    -> status: Status.SUCCESS
   177→  - initial state    -> status: Status.IDLE
   178→
   179→Benefits:
   180→  - Single source of truth for state
   181→  - Cleaner conditional logic
   182→  - Easier to add new states (e.g., REFRESHING)
   183→
   184→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   185→
   186→## 3. Callback Handling in Rescue Domain
   187→
   188→LEARNING: Redux rescue action accepts callbacks (onSuccess, onFailed) in the action payload.
   189→These callbacks were handled by Redux-Saga middleware.
   190→
   191→SOLUTION: In Zustand, handle callbacks in the SERVICE layer, not the store.
   192→
   193→```typescript
   194→// rescue.service.ts
   195→export async function performRescue(
   196→  payload: RescuePayload,
   197→  callbacks?: RescueCallbacks
   198→): Promise<void> {
   199→  try {
   200→    rescueActions.setStatus(RescueStatus.LOADING);
   201→    const response = await rescueApi.rescue(payload);
   202→
   203→    if (response?.data?.status === 'OK') {
   204→      rescueActions.setStatus(RescueStatus.SUCCESS);
   205→      callbacks?.onSuccess?.(response.status);  // Callback here
   206→    } else {
   207→      rescueActions.setStatus(RescueStatus.ERROR);
   208→      callbacks?.onFailed?.(response?.status);   // Callback here
   209→    }
   210→  } catch (error) {
   211→    rescueActions.setStatus(RescueStatus.ERROR);
   212→    callbacks?.onFailed?.();
   213→  }
   214→}
   215→```

   223→  2. setRewardsProgramSlices function
   224→  3. clearRewardsProgramStore cleanup function

   243→Missing any slice will cause state persistence issues across sessions.
   244→

   247→export function clearRewardsProgramStore() {

# Reward Program - Redux to Zustand Migration

Last Updated: 2026-01-26 18:45
Status: COMPLETED

================================================================================
PROJECT CONTEXT
================================================================================

Package: @semparar/mf-app-fintech-rewardprogram v0.0.1
Type: React Native microfrontend for Reward Program
Consumer: SuperApp (appSemPararv3)
State: Hybrid - Zustand (new) + Redux (legacy)
Pattern: Hook Component (index.ts + view.tsx)

Key Rule: NO LOGIC CHANGES - production code, only adapt structure to Zustand

================================================================================
CURRENT ZUSTAND DOMAINS (7 Complete)
================================================================================

| Domain       | API | Model | Repository | Service | Store | Hook |
|--------------|-----|-------|------------|---------|-------|------|
| airlineMiles | Yes | Yes   | -          | Yes     | Yes   | Yes  |
| extract      | Yes | Yes   | Yes        | Yes     | Yes   | -    |
| parameter    | Yes | Yes   | Yes        | Yes     | Yes   | -    |
| permission   | Yes | Yes   | Yes        | Yes     | Yes   | Yes  |
| products     | Yes | Yes   | Yes        | Yes     | Yes   | -    |  <- NEW
| rescue       | Yes | Yes   | -          | Yes     | Yes   | Yes  |  <- NEW

Files in src/services/:

        products.api.ts, rescue.api.ts, marketplace.api.ts
  models/: airlineMiles.model.ts, extract.model.ts, parameter.model.ts, permission.model.ts,
           products.model.ts, rescue.model.ts, marketplace.model.ts

               products.repository.ts
  services/: airlineMiles.service.ts, extract.service.ts, parameter.service.ts, permission.service.ts,
             products.service.ts, rescue.service.ts, marketplace.service.ts

          products.store.ts, rescue.store.ts, marketplace.store.ts, slices.ts

  utils/: cleanup.ts, participantId.ts

================================================================================
COMPLETED TASKS
================================================================================

src/services/store/slices.ts
